zh.lifeSummary: 當George de Beaumont Cole-Orton於1452年出生在英國越可爾坦普林福薩姆時，他的父親Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB43歲，母親Johanna Darcy27歲。他與Johanna Pauncefort在英國萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1514年3月19日在英國萊希斯特斯爾享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George de Beaumont Cole-Orton于1452年出生在英国越可爾坦普林福薩姆时，他的父亲Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB43岁，母亲Johanna Darcy27岁。他与Johanna Pauncefort在英国萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1514年3月19日在英国萊希斯特斯爾享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When George de Beaumont Cole-Orton was born in 1452, in Templenewsam, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB, was 43 and his mother, Johanna Darcy, was 27. He married Johanna Pauncefort in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 19 March 1514, in Leicestershire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George de Beaumont Cole-Orton est né en 1452, à Templenewsam, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Johanna Darcy, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Johanna Pauncefort à Coleorton, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 19 mars 1514, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als George de Beaumont Cole-Orton 1452 in Templenewsam, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB 43 und seine Mutter Johanna Darcy 27. Er hat in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England Johanna Pauncefort geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 19. März 1514 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Leicestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George de Beaumont Cole-Orton è nato il nel 1452 nel Templenewsam, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Darcy, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Johanna Pauncefort nel Coleorton, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 19 marzo 1514, nel Leicestershire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: George de Beaumont Cole-Ortonが1452年にイギリスヨークシュアイアテンプリーネウサムで生まれた時，父Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KBは43歳，母Johanna Darcyは27歳であった。イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコーレオルトンでJohanna Pauncefortと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1514年3月19日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: George de Beaumont Cole-Orton(이)가 1452년 영국 요르크실르 템렌w샘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Darcy(은)는 27세였다. 그는 영국 레이스스터실르 콜리르턴에서 Johanna Pauncefort(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1514년 3월 19일 영국 레이스스터실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George de Beaumont Cole-Orton nasceu em 1452, em Templenewsam, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Darcy, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Johanna Pauncefort em Coleorton, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 19 de março de 1514, em Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George de Beaumont Cole-Orton родился в 1452 г. в местности темплинюсэм, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Johanna Darcy, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna Pauncefort в местности колеортон, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 19 марта 1514 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George de Beaumont Cole-Orton nació en 1452, en Templenewsam, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Johanna Darcy, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Johanna Pauncefort en Coleorton, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 19 de marzo de 1514, en Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
